articles.title: President Trump Promises Navy Help for Ships in the Middle East
articles.summary: President Donald Trump says the US Navy will protect ships in the Middle East if needed. This is because there is a big problem with oil and gas supplies. A place called the Strait of Hormuz is very important because one-fifth of the world's oil and gas goes through it. But Iran has threatened to attack ships there, so many ships have stopped moving. Trump wants to make sure energy can flow freely to the world. He also says the US will offer cheap insurance to shipping companies to help them feel safe. Experts are not sure if this will work because companies are still worried. Oil prices went down a little after Trump's announcement, but people are concerned about high prices for gas and other things.

articles.title: TV Stars Ask Judge for Help in Art Money Mystery
articles.summary: Ant and Dec are famous TV hosts who love collecting art by Banksy, a well-known street artist. They bought and sold some Banksy artworks with the help of a consultant, but they think something went wrong with the money. They paid £550,000 for six prints, but the seller only got £300,000, so £250,000 is missing. They also sold 22 items and believe they lost more money. Now, they have gone to court to ask a judge to help them find out where the missing money went. They want an art dealer to share information about the deals. The art dealer says he did nothing wrong and was just buying art fairly. This is a story about trust and making sure people are honest in business.

articles.title: A Newspaper Writer Says She Didn't Listen to Phone Messages
articles.summary: A former newspaper writer named Nicole Lampert says she did not listen to private phone messages of actors Jude Law and Sadie Frost. She wrote stories about their relationship, but she says she got the information from a good source who was close to Sadie Frost. Sadie Frost told the court that she now believes her phone messages were hacked to get stories about her. She and other famous people are suing the newspaper company for invading their privacy over many years. The court is hearing about articles from 2003 to 2005. Lampert says she never hacked phones and that a freelance journalist gave her the information. The newspaper had to say sorry for one wrong story about money in the divorce. This case is about whether the newspaper did something wrong by getting private details without permission.

articles.title: How the Spring Statement Affects Your Money
articles.summary: The UK government has shared new forecasts about the economy in the Spring Statement. These numbers help us understand what might happen to your money. Inflation, which is how fast prices go up, is expected to be around 2% over the next five years. This is much lower than before, so the Bank of England might lower interest rates, making loans cheaper but savings earn less. However, conflicts in the Middle East could change this. Also, average disposable income, the money people have after taxes, is predicted to grow slowly each year from 2026 to 2030, reaching about £26,900 per person by 2030. But tax thresholds are frozen until 2031, meaning pay rises could push people into higher tax brackets. House prices are another thing to watch for homeowners or buyers.

articles.title: Scientists Use Light to Flip a Magnet
articles.summary: Scientists from two universities in Switzerland have found a new way to change a magnet's direction using only a laser light. Magnets work because tiny parts inside them, called spins, all point the same way, like a team working together. Usually, to flip a magnet, you need to heat it up to make the spins move around and then cool it down so they point a new way. But these scientists did it with light without heating, which is a big breakthrough. They used a special material made of thin layers twisted slightly. This lets them control the magnet with light, which could help make faster and smaller electronics in the future. The magnet is very small, only a few micrometers wide, and they checked it flipped by shining another light on it and seeing how it reflected.

articles.title: How Scientists Use Special Tools to Make Fusion Energy Work
articles.summary: Fusion energy is a way to make clean power by combining tiny particles, like the sun does. To make it work safely, scientists need to watch superhot plasma inside machines. They use tools called diagnostics to measure how hot and dense the plasma is. A new report from the U.S. Department of Energy says we should invest more in these tools. This report came from a workshop where experts from labs and universities met to decide which tools are most important. They want to help the U.S. lead in fusion energy and build power plants by the 2030s. The report talks about seven key areas in plasma science and suggests making tougher sensors, faster measurements, and using AI to design better tools. It also says we need more trained scientists to work on this. By improving diagnostics, we can make fusion energy a reality faster.
